title: Reveal on hover via mask
reference_inspiration: No external reference is required; the interaction is specified by duration, easing, state change, and reduced-motion behavior in this row.
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. clip-path or mask transition.
solution:
.reveal { clip-path: inset(0 100% 0 0); transition: clip-path 0.5s ease; }
.reveal:hover { clip-path: inset(0 0 0 0); }
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.

title: Logo hover wiggle
reference_inspiration: No external reference is required; the interaction is specified by duration, easing, state change, and reduced-motion behavior in this row.
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Single-shot rotation.
solution:
.logo:hover { animation: wiggle 0.5s ease; }
@keyframes wiggle { 25% {transform: rotate(-3deg);} 50% {transform: rotate(3deg);} 75% {transform: rotate(-2deg);} }
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.

title: Animated bottom tab badge
reference_inspiration: No external reference is required; the interaction is specified by duration, easing, state change, and reduced-motion behavior in this row.
motion_reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Scale 0→1 on appear; spring.
solution:
const scale = useSharedValue(0);
useEffect(() => { scale.value = count > 0 ? withSpring(1) : withSpring(0); }, [count]);
what_breaks_naive_approach: A visual-only animation that ignores reduced motion, cleanup, focus, or layout cost can look fine in a demo while causing jank, inaccessible state changes, or orphaned timers/tweens in production.

title: Image lightbox open animation
reference_inspiration: No external reference is required; the interaction is specified by duration, easing, state change, and reduced-motion behavior in this row.
motion_reasoning: The motion should communicate the state change directly, stay under a short perceptual threshold, preserve layout stability, and provide a reduced-motion fallback.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Only shrinking duration to 0.01ms can still repeat an animation many times and cause flicker; globally removing everything can hide important state transitions.
why_this_is_correct: The reset prevents infinite or repeated animations from continuing under reduced-motion preferences while preserving instantaneous state changes. Scoped overrides keep essential affordances visible.
